title: Molten Tunisian Millet Flatbread
description: Earthy, molten, comforting Tunisian dish featuring palm sugar, long-fermentation-dough, macrobiotic-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together millet flour, whole wheat flour, salt, instant yeast, and dried thyme.
Add warm water and olive oil, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
Transfer dough to a lightly oiled container, cover, and let it ferment at room temperature for 12 hours.
After fermentation, gently knead the dough for 2 minutes to release gas.
Divide the dough into four equal portions and roll each into a thin oval flatbread about 1 cm thick.
Sprinkle each flatbread lightly with crumbled palm sugar and a pinch of cumin.
Place the flatbreads on a parchment-lined baking sheet and bake for 9 minutes.
Remove from oven and let cool for 2 minutes while the sugar melts into a glossy glaze.
Serve warm with a side of harissa or a simple tomato salad.
